justification: All 7 rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions rule (BR-5) correctly marked invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit surfaced as an escalation. 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level 72 is within the 60-85 range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 business rules identified and correctly classified as invariant/non-invariant
Notification rule correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification
Sanctions screening rule included as invariant: true with regulatory rationale
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced as an escalation with three options and a recommendation
13 acceptance criteria far exceeds the 4 minimum, with edge cases like exact $1,000 boundary and consecutive login reset
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified limits and flags the withdrawal ambiguity
Excellent edge cases including race conditions in login lockout, sanctions list propagation delay, and mid-day tier downgrade
Confidence level 72 is well-calibrated given the ambiguities identified
BR-2 and BR-3 marked invariant: false — the existence of daily limits is arguably an invariant even if specific amounts are configurable, though this is a defensible interpretation
Strong business impact analysis with specific stakeholder identification and risk prioritization
